Three hand-defined read-only governance tools (get_current_status, get_current_charter, list_recent_sessions) dispatched via blocking httpx against the local REST API. Throwaway code — Slice B is the first slice with production code. Build executed in this Claude Code conversation against WT-048 (the Slice-A kickoff body authored under SES-080 / CONV-050). Records landed: 1 session (SES-087), 1 conversation (CONV-057), 1 commit (b519df0… → CM-0008 lazy), 0 new work_tickets, 0 new planning items, 0 decisions (per kickoff: Slice A is execution against DEC-261, no architectural decisions expected — the loop composed exactly as the design doc predicted), 4 payload references (1 is_about + 3 already in conversation embedded edges), 1 addresses edge (CONV-057 → PI-052). WT-048 consumed at apply close via PATCH (apply_close_out.py does not auto-transition; pattern matches SES-080 close-out apply). PI-052 remains Open — resolves on Slice D close. Identifier rebasing per the parallel-sandbox-collision discipline: kickoff anticipated SES-081, but SES-081 through SES-086 were consumed by parallel PI-003/PI-004 work; rebased to SES-087.

session.summary: PI-052 Slice A shipped. Foundational architecture validated: Anthropic SDK + native tools=[...] + blocking httpx against the local REST API compose into a working chat loop in ~130 LOC of pure Python, with no surprises in tool-use block shape, no surprises in the multi-turn tool dispatch protocol, and no surprises in the envelope-unwrap path. The lesson from DEC-244 (verify end-to-end before building infrastructure) holds: if the spike had not worked, DEC-257's threading model and DEC-261's downstream slices would have been built on a foundation that doesn't exist. It worked. Slice B (PySide6 MVP tab, streaming on, one tool wired through dispatcher) is unblocked. The script lives at crmbuilder-v2/scripts/chat_spike.py as a reference; it is throwaway code, not maintained going forward — Slice C does the refactor to mcp_server.tools.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: PI-052 Slice A — terminal proof-of-concept for Anthropic-API chat loop with three read-only governance tools
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: PI-052 Slice A — terminal proof-of-concept for Anthropic-API chat loop with three read-only governance tools

WT-048. Per DEC-261 (slicing plan) and DEC-257 (streaming on for the
production UI — explicitly deferred for this spike), this is the
foundational architecture validation: a ~130 LOC Python script that runs
a blocking chat loop with three hand-defined tools (get_current_status,
get_current_charter, list_recent_sessions), validating that the
Anthropic SDK + native tools=[...] + httpx against the local REST API
compose into a working chat loop before any PySide6 code lands.

No PySide6, no streaming, no caching, no persistence. Slice B is the
first slice with production code. The script is throwaway —
committed as a reference but not maintained.

Implementation notes:
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env required; prints to stderr and exits 1 if missing
  (no keyring path per DEC-254 — Slice B adds that).
- Three tools defined inline as JSON Schema in the TOOLS list. No reuse
  of mcp_server.tools (Slice C does the refactor per design Appendix A).
- httpx.Client (blocking) against http://127.0.0.1:8765 with 30s timeout.
- Multi-tool turn handled in the inner while loop — collects every
  tool_use block in one assistant message, dispatches each, appends a
  single tool_result message with the full batch.
- Tool call echo to stderr (one line per call: → name(args)).
- Final assistant text printed to stdout once stop_reason != tool_use.
- Ctrl+C and EOF both exit cleanly with code 0.
- Tool errors caught and returned as is_error tool_result blocks so the
  assistant sees them rather than the script crashing.

Smoke-tested the three dispatch paths against the live REST API
(returned status singleton, charter singleton, recent sessions list
with default-3 and explicit limit=2). The live Anthropic chat loop
test (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY required) is Doug's manual gate per the
kickoff acceptance criteria.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
